name: Winterbourne Gunner
overview: Winterbourne Gunner is a village in the Bourne valley near the A338, about 4 miles north-east of Salisbury, adjoining Winterbourne Dauntsey. A former parish in its own right, in 1934 it merged with Winterbourne Earls and Winterbourne Dauntsey to form the civil parish of Winterbourne. The River Bourne, a winterbourne that often dries in summer, runs through the village, and its parish church is St Mary the Virgin.
character: A small, quiet flint-and-cob village at the northern end of the Winterbournes, more rural and dispersed than its neighbours, with open downland close by.
amenities: St Mary the Virgin parish church
Winterbourne Earls CE Primary School in the adjoining village
The Winterbourne Arms pub at nearby Winterbourne Dauntsey
Bourne Valley community groups and events
No village shop - everyday shopping is in Salisbury

schools.name: Winterbourne Earls Church of England Primary School

commutes.notes: About 4 miles south-west via the A338 for shops, services and the station
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m
Buses run along the A338 Bourne valley corridor

prices.summary: Stock is predominantly period cottages and detached houses with some later infill, in low volumes given the village's small size; the quiet rural setting and Salisbury proximity support solid values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking along the River Bourne and out onto the surrounding chalk downland
Following the Roman Port Way towards Old Sarum
Joining Bourne Valley community clubs and seasonal events
placesToEat: Community cafe events run across the Winterbournes
pros: Peaceful, rural northern end of the Bourne valley
Good-rated primary school in the adjoining village
About 4 miles from Salisbury's full range of services
cons: No shop or pub within Winterbourne Gunner itself
The Bourne can flood the lower ground in wet winters
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a quiet, rural valley village with downland walking, within an easy drive of Salisbury and a Good-rated primary nearby.

sources.label: Wiltshire OPC Project - Winterbourne Gunner (St Mary's history)
Ofsted - Winterbourne Earls CE Primary (URN 151736, Good, May 2024)
